page: Vultr voc-s-32c-256gb-5760s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 5760 GB local NVMe SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark results indicate strong capabilities across memory and compute tasks. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth for both read and write operations. Multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall within the top 25% tier. Redis SET operations also demonstrate strong performance, while static web serving and LLM inference speeds for medium-sized models remain in the average tier.

The CPU allocation is shared, which introduces potential performance variability under sustained multi-tenant workloads, representing a key tradeoff for high-density compute tasks. However, this shared architecture combined with a high memory-to-core ratio and substantial local NVMe storage provides excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. This server is highly suited for large-scale databases, memory-cached applications, data indexing, and high-capacity storage-bound services.
description: The Vultr voc-s-32c-256gb-5760s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 256.0 GB of RAM. It includes a substantial 5760 GB local NVMe SSD but lacks a dedicated GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in single-core CPU execution and large-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core and cryptographic processing. Because the CPU allocation is shared, users may experience performance variability under heavy concurrent neighbor loads. However, the high memory-to-core ratio and massive local storage deliver excellent resource density and qualitative cost efficiency. This server is well-suited for large databases, caching layers, and storage-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-32c-256gb-5760s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 5760 GB of NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-32c-256gb-5760s-amd is an AMD EPYC server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 5760 GB NVMe storage, optimized for memory and database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity x86_64 server combining 256 GB of RAM with 5.7 TB of NVMe storage for memory-intensive and database workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC architecture with 32 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM (1:8 ratio)
Generous 5760 GB local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier single-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks
Shared CPU allocation offering high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency
Best suited for large-scale databases, caching layers, and data indexing
